meanings.definition: a small amount of liquid food

examples: one swallow of the liquid was enough
he took a drink of his beer and smacked his lips

meanings.definition: small long-winged songbird noted for swift graceful flight and the regularity of its migrations
pass through the esophagus as part of eating or drinking
examples: Swallow the raw fish--it won't kill you!
The Nazis swallowed the Baltic countries

meanings.definition: enclose or envelop completely, as if by swallowing
examples: The huge waves swallowed the small boat and it sank shortly thereafter
She swallowed the last words of her speech

meanings.definition: take back what one has said

examples: I swallowed my anger and kept quiet
I shall have to accept these unpleasant working conditions
She has learned to live with her husband's little idiosyncrasies

meanings.definition: believe or accept without questioning or challenge
examples: Am I supposed to swallow that story?
